calls.title: Deyaar Refresher Training 01
calls.summary: First of two refresher sessions for Deyaar focused on re-grounding users on Facilio's WO, inspection and asset flows. Adoption gaps tied to management changes; manual WO and findings-driven flow confirmed as canonical.
key_points: Goal: digitize maintenance & operations on Facilio for better visibility and data access.
Old work-order backlog reduced by deletion, but volume remains high; automatic ticket generation from inspections has been disabled in favour of manual WO creation.
Walked through mobile app flow for work-order assignment, site visits and closure.
Reviewed asset management — portfolio structure, search/filter, asset details.
Inspections now drive 'findings' that lead to work orders rather than auto-generating them.
Permissions and user roles are blocking some users; visibility/access issues being tracked.
decisions: Manual WO creation is the standard going forward; auto-generation from inspections stays off.
Findings-based flow is the canonical path from inspection → corrective WO.

action_items.task: Drive adoption — regular system utilization for KPI & vendor mgmt.
Address permission/visibility issues raised by users.

risks: Work-order volume still high; permission gaps blocking some user actions.
open_items: Permission/role fixes required; user adoption depends on training landing.

calls.title: Facilio <> ES GLOBAL UAT Session
calls.summary: UAT walkthrough with ES Global covering client portal, WO approval chain, quotation/SLA mechanics and material flow. Clear next steps on user provisioning and training plan.
key_points: Demoed client portal — request creation, tracking dashboards for SRs, WOs and quotations.
WO approval chain walked through: admin → supervisor → FM engineer.
Quotation flow: vendor/staff assignment, line items, VAT at subtotal, digital signatures.
Clarified SLA behaviour — pauses during quote approval, resumes on client approval.
Material request flow refined to prioritize supervisor input; storekeeper owns inventory.
Vendor workflow includes quote, work-permit checklist and approval before commencement.
decisions: Rejection comments mandatory across all rejection states.
FM engineer can now edit quotes directly instead of reject-and-resend.
UAT access via email IDs, Jira for feedback tracking.

action_items.task: Provision UAT users by email ID and stand up Jira project for feedback.
Plan technician and client training cadence.

risks: Standardising rejection-comment behaviour; material-request handover between supervisor and storekeeper.
open_items: UAT user list from ES Global; Jira project setup.

calls.title: AlShalfa - Session 4
calls.summary: Session 4 closed out several config items (KPIs, QR codes, PPM, print layout) but flagged geotagging timeline as open and pushed back on the client's maintenance-history report as needing a separate module.
key_points: Client-provided formulas for KPIs implemented; temporary KPI tab to be removed.
QR code generation for assets now available to all users.
PPM configs refined — asset condition linked to SLAs; rework/evaluation flow improved.
Comprehensive maintenance-history report not directly feasible from existing WO data.
WO print layout customizable up to 12 fields.
Arabic language support limited to field/module names; statuses stay in English.
Geotagging features under development with no firm timeline.
Bulk approval confirmed via client portal.
decisions: Temporary KPI tab to be removed once the new KPI formulas are validated.
Maintenance-history reporting will require a separate module, not WO-data extension.

action_items.task: Validate client KPI formulas and remove temporary KPI tab.
Scope a separate module for maintenance-history reporting.

risks: Geotagging delivery uncertainty; maintenance-history report scope creep.
open_items: Client-side validation of KPI formulas; further scoping for history reports module.

calls.title: Placeholder - Facilio <> KSD Integration Workshop
calls.summary: Deep-dive integration workshop covering KSD's Sage flows and the differing client systems (Verisign, Nvidia, Tesco/Sainsbury's/Argos). Significant integration uncertainty hinges on third-party vendor engagement.
key_points: Integration scope: KSD financial & ops processes with Facilio + Sage (invoicing, POs, client/vendor mgmt).
Need clarified API documentation and sandbox access from Verisign / Nvidia.
Detailed walkthrough of Verisign and Nvidia flows for accept/decline/quote/invoice.
Standardized dropdowns needed across Tesco, Sainsbury's, Argos for reasons/codes.
Material invoicing for internal engineers identified as complex, needs further work.
PPM vs reactive WO invoicing differs sharply between Verisign and Nvidia.
decisions: Sequencing of integrations will follow vendor responsiveness — Nvidia likely first.
Separate follow-up with Christina to scope H&S and audits requirements.

action_items.task: Drive vendor (Nvidia/Verisign) towards API access & sandbox.
Map Sage coding & VAT structures to Facilio invoice flow.
Share full list of client-specific dropdown values.

risks: Third-party API responsiveness; material-invoicing complexity; per-client dropdown standardization.
open_items: API/sandbox access from Nvidia & Verisign; Sage coding map; H&S audit scoping follow-up with Christina.

calls.title: Facilio Osus Discussion
calls.summary: Osus session surfaced two material risks: client wants visibility into license consumption against MSA (no dashboard today) and phase-1 go-live is blocked on internal UAT sign-off. Tenant/asset mapping demos went well.
key_points: Operational topics: integrating inspection quotations into WO; plan for UAT and go-live with FM team.
Clarified difference between roles and licenses for admin users.
Concerns raised that there's no dashboard / validation to track license consumption vs MSA.
Vendor portal license limit = 100; client/tenant limit = 500.
Confirmed rule-based vs named-user license distinction.
Sandbox demo for tenant space and asset mapping; to be replicated in production.
Need formal UAT sign-off for phase-1 to proceed with go-live.
decisions: License management is rule-based, not strictly license-number-based for internal users.
Sandbox tenant/asset mapping flow will be replicated in production.

action_items.task: Provide phase-1 UAT sign-off confirmation after internal checks.
Investigate custom dashboard/report for license consumption tracking.
Resolve technician account activation & password reset procedures.

risks: No license-consumption dashboard; technician activation friction; UAT sign-off lag.
open_items: Client internal checks for phase-1 UAT sign-off; Facilio scoping of license dashboard.

calls.title: Edarat - Work Shop - 1
calls.summary: First requirements workshop with Edarat anchored hierarchy, SR vs WO distinction, corrective WO workflow and material request approvals. Process diagrams owed by Arun for next session.
key_points: Mapped hierarchy: Site → Building → Floor → Space → Independent Space → Subspace.
Reactive maintenance via Service Requests; corrective work flows into Work Orders.
Defined SR workflow with client portal access, helpdesk role, classification, and client approval before closure.
Corrective WO workflow: supervisor decides in-house vs vendor; technician/field engineer roles spelled out.
Material request: technician planning → supervisor review → field engineer request → multi-level approvals (FE, Supervisor, DC Manager).
Warranty checks differentiate equipment warranty vs spare-part shelf life.
TFM-related SRs require a technician inspection step before resolving or converting to WO.
decisions: TFM SRs will include a mandatory inspection step before resolution/conversion.
Material request approval chain confirmed (FE → Supervisor → DC Manager).

action_items.task: Provide process flow diagrams for SR and WO workflows.
Continue scoping material request and PTW processes.

risks: Material request approval chain complexity; PTW process still to be scoped.
open_items: Process flow diagrams from Arun; client clarification on PTW.

calls.title: Deyaar Refresher Training 02
calls.summary: Second refresher confirmed Facilio as the canonical FM system at Deyaar but surfaced real mobile-app performance issues, permit workflow gaps and inspection-report bugs that have to be cleared to land adoption.
key_points: Goal: Facilio becomes the sole system for WO and facility management tasks.
Two Facilio apps — vendor and workstation — assigned by role.
WO lifecycle covered end-to-end including material requirements, proposals and procurement integration.
Mobile app performance and UI concerns raised; commitment to investigate with mobile team.
Work permit mgmt — document collection and customization still has gaps.
Inspection reporting issues: duplicate entries, missing evidential photos.
Auto-assignment and streamlined closure flagged as needed for efficiency.
decisions: Facilio to be the canonical system for WO and FM tasks — full transition path agreed.

action_items.task: Investigate mobile app performance & UI issues raised.
Address inspection reporting bugs (duplicates, photo evidence).
Implement auto-assignment for WOs and streamline closure flow.
Coordinate user access and asset data management.

risks: Mobile UX; permit workflow gaps; inspection-report accuracy; WO auto-assignment.
open_items: Mobile team triage; DR IT for user/asset data; permit process redesign.

calls.title: [Lunds & Byerlys x Facilio] RM Touchbase
calls.summary: Touchbase refined condenser approach rule (ambient + fan speed, 3-hr persistence, defrost exclusions) and aligned on a 2-store pilot focused on alarms before predictive features.
key_points: Refining condenser approach rule — ambient temp + fan speed instead of simple differential.
New alarm logic: ambient < limit AND fan speed > limit, persistence of 3 hours, to avoid false positives.
Periods with heavy defrost activity (>20-30%) excluded from condenser alarm logic.
'Connect to device' to be integrated into the maintenance app for unified UX.
Refrigerant compliance and asset naming gaps in the new app under investigation.
Rollout will start with 2 pilot stores; alarms first, predictive rules later.
decisions: Two pilot stores for initial rollout, focused on alarms.
Defrost-heavy windows excluded from condenser alarm rule.

action_items.task: Productionise updated condenser rule and validate on pilot stores.
Publish weekly alarm report (RDM-style) with improved data aggregation.
Embed 'connect to device' into maintenance app via work order.

risks: New app refrigerant-compliance & asset naming issues need workflow adjustments.
open_items: Pilot store readiness; mobile app rework for 'connect to device' inside WOs.

calls.title: UK Lifts & Escalator - Weekly Cadence
calls.summary: Weekly cadence cleared a stack of quote-builder and vendor-onboarding items and accepted a new annual pre-invoicing flow. Finance module testing flagged as critical before go-live.
key_points: Quote builder: separate total labor & material charges; 'trip allowance' → 'travel rates'.
Rounding logic for base charges and travel/labor rates implemented.
Annual pre-invoicing workflow before contract activation proposed and accepted.
Inspection checklist integration into contracts and vendor onboarding complete.
Vendor rates (travel, labor) now visible during the vendor approval process.
Vendor quote → client quote conversion with markups demonstrated.
Renaming 'vendor' → 'subcontractor' deemed not feasible (system naming).
decisions: Annual pre-invoicing workflow accepted — to be productionised.
Vendor↔client quote linkage to be added for traceability.

action_items.task: Schedule finance module testing ahead of go-live (called out as critical).
Add vendor↔client quote linkage and vendor job-status visibility.
Explore flexible appointment scheduling for inspection checklists.

risks: Finance module testing critical before go-live; naming convention friction.
open_items: Finance testing slot; pilot of pre-invoicing workflow.

calls.title: AMBK x Facilio - Connected Buildings Demonstration
calls.summary: Connected Buildings demo for AMBK covering data integration, energy, FDD and ESG. Partner asked for a tailored deck and solution menu to take to their warehouse/office/manufacturing clients.
key_points: Data integration via APIs, BACnet, Modbus and OCR for warehouses, offices, manufacturing.
Digital twins, KPI tracking, energy savings insights, occupant comfort use cases.
Tenant energy apportionment, weather-normalized baselining, benchmarking, scoring.
FDD: suboptimal operation detection, alarms with estimated cost/energy impact.
ESG reporting — Scope 1 & 2 direct emissions, with path to indirect emissions.
Integration with AMBK's MS Dynamics FSM confirmed as a priority via API.
decisions: AMBK to receive a tailored sales presentation + 'solution menu' to pitch onwards.
MS Dynamics FSM integration treated as priority for any AMBK client engagement.

action_items.task: Build tailored AMBK sales deck and solution menu.
Share solved use cases and customer case studies.

risks: Downstream user hesitancy in AMBK's target clients; need to anchor economic benefit.
open_items: AMBK to provide target client list; Facilio to deliver tailored materials & case studies.

calls.title: Module Demo & Config Session (Adhithyan)
calls.summary: Configuration walkthrough across PM, safety plans, dashboards, inspections, shift/attendance and assets. Auto-WO from failed inspections explicitly deferred to Phase 2; employee module to be renamed 'Users'.
key_points: Demoed planned maintenance setup, WO generation and PM calendar.
Safety plans explained — distinct from job plan tasks.
Reviewed supervisor / technician / occupant dashboards.
Inspections configurable; auto WO from failed inspections deferred to Phase 2.
Shift & attendance demoed for check-in/check-out tracking.
Asset module — parent-child relationships and data import shown.
Employee module to be renamed to 'Users'.
Resolved past UAT feedback: UI fixes, sorting, mandatory resolution comments.
decisions: Auto-WO-from-failed-inspection deferred to Phase 2.
Employee module to be renamed to 'Users'.

action_items.task: Push UI fixes, sorting and mandatory-comment changes from prior UAT.
Plan Phase 2 enhancement for auto-WO from failed inspections.

risks: Space-category mapping for PMs; onboarding flow for users/assets.
